name: Q S Bus Inc

fit_notes: Very small Ft. Lauderdale operator (~38 employees, ~$1.7M revenue) with virtually no web presence. Too small to be a meaningful platform target; could be a micro tuck-in if it holds Broward/Miami-Dade routes, but data quality is poor and warrants direct outreach to confirm operation.

name: J & M Transport
districts_or_customers: Likely Hamburg Area SD or Berks County IU 14 contractor; not confirmed
recent_news: No public news found; numerous similarly-named freight carriers complicate identification
fit_notes: Small Berks County PA operator (~41 employees, ~$1.7M revenue). No website, name collides with multiple freight LLCs. If it is the Hamburg Area SD contractor, it would be a micro tuck-in in Summit's PA footprint, but needs phone confirmation before pursuing.

name: Big Sky Bus
districts_or_customers: Casino/leisure tour passengers (Coeur d'Alene Casino Resort weekend trips); NOT a school bus operator
recent_news: Operates as tour/charter company focused on casino getaway packages
fit_notes: NOT A TUCK-IN TARGET. Big Sky Bus Tours is a charter/casino tour operator in Helena, MT, not a student transportation contractor. Distinct from Helena Public Schools transportation. Disqualify.

name: Fischer Transportation Inc
principals: Mary Kay Adamski (Ray Fischer's daughter) and John Fischer (Ray's son) co-owners; Patsy Fischer long-tenured family operator (since 1974)
districts_or_customers: Edgar School District, Marathon School District (since 2001), Athens School District (since 2013)
recent_news: 2024: Partnered with Edgar SD and Navistar to deploy first electric school bus via EPA Clean School Bus grant
fit_notes: Classic family-owned tuck-in profile: ~38-bus fleet, three contiguous central WI districts (Edgar/Marathon/Athens), 80+ year operating history (founded mid-1940s by Ray Fischer), 2nd-generation owners with 40+ years tenure, and proven EV grant capability. High succession signal given founder generation aging. Note: source data lists 'Richard Dahl' as owner — not corroborated by public sources, which consistently name Fischer family members; verify before outreach. WI is outside core Summit footprint but viable expansion-state tuck-in.

name: Central Avenue Bus Svc
principals: Rev. Norris A. Jones (founder; school teacher and pastor)
districts_or_customers: DC-area schools, businesses and organizations; owns school buses since 2015; field trip and daily route services
recent_news: No recent M&A or growth news identified
fit_notes: Very small (~23 employees, ~$1.2M revenue) founder-led DC operator established 1989 by Rev. Norris Jones. School bus operations only since 2015, so limited scale. High succession signal given founder profile and age of business, but too small alone to move the needle; could be a DCPS-foothold micro-tuck-in if Summit wants to enter the DC contract market. DC is outside listed Summit footprint.
